data.title: Hold Rating Reaffirmed as Strong EBITDA and Buyer Engagement Are Offset by Weak Marketplace Trends and Subdued Near-Term Outlook
data.description:
Needham analyst Bernie McTernan has maintained their neutral stance on FVRR stock, giving a Hold rating on April 27.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bernie McTernan has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including stronger-than-expected adjusted EBITDA in 1Q26 that was supported by improved margins and a solid upside in services revenue. That strength, however, was partially driven by an earlier-than-planned recognition of AutoDS revenue and contrasted with a notable year-over-year decline in marketplace revenue, even though larger-ticket transactions showed healthy double-digit growth.

He also points out that annual spend per active buyer continues to rise at a mid-teens rate, signaling deeper engagement among existing users, but this positive is tempered by a subdued near-term top-line outlook. Management’s second-quarter guidance calls for revenue slightly below market expectations, and while the full-year revenue forecast was reaffirmed and the EBITDA outlook modestly improved at the low end, the overall risk‑reward profile appears balanced rather than compelling, supporting a neutral, or Hold, stance on the shares.

In another report released on April 27, RBC Capital also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $16.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
